channel.title: Ezekiel on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Ezekiel on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Ezekiel 1
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 1

1 The fifth day, fourth month, thirtieth year, I was by the Chebar River with the exiles. The sky opened up, and I saw a vision of God. ( 2 The fifth month, fifth year of Jehoiachin’s exile, 3 God’s message came to Ezekiel, and God’s hand came on him.) 4 A strong north wind was blowing, and a large cloud with a bright glow around it flashed constantly with lightning. In the cloud was something like glowing metal 5 and four figures like living beings with human forms. 6 Each one had four faces and four wings. 7 They had straight legs, and their feet resembled calf hooves that shined like polished bronze. 8 There were hands under their wings, 9 and the wings of all four touched each other. Their faces didn’t turn as they moved straight forward. 10 Each one had the faces of a man, an eagle, a lion on the right, and a bull on the left. 11 Their wings spread out above them, two wings touching the wings of the next living beings and two covering their bodies. 12 They went straight forward wherever the spirit went, without turning as they went. 13 Among the beings were what looked like burning coals, like torches moving back and forth. The fire burned bright, and lightning kept flashing out of it. 14 The living beings darted back and forth like bolts of lightning.

item.title: Ezekiel 2
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 2

Ezekiel’s Call

1 “Son of man, stand up so I can talk to you!” 2 When the Spirit stood me up, 3 he said,

“I’m sending you to the Israelites, a rebellious people. They and their ancestors have been sinning against me to this day. 4 Tell those impudent, stubborn children, ‘The Lord GOD says.’ 5 Whether they listen or not, they’ll know a prophet’s been there. 6 Don’t be afraid of them or intimidated by what they say, though you’re surrounded by briars and thorns and sitting on scorpions. 7 Tell them what I’ve said whether they listen or not.

item.title: Ezekiel 3
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 3

[4 ] 5-6 “I’m not sending you to people that speak a hard foreign language you can’t understand. I’m sending you to people that should listen; 7 but they won’t listen to you, because they won’t listen to me. The whole house is stubborn. 8 I’ve made your face and forehead as hard as theirs, 9 like corundum, harder than flint. So don’t be afraid of them or be intimidated by them though they’re rebellious people. 10 Listen closely and take everything to heart that I tell you. 11 Go to your people in exile and tell them, ‘The Lord GOD says,’ whether they listen or not.”

item.title: Ezekiel 4
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 4

Ezek 3:22-27

Siege of Jerusalem Symbolized

1 “Now, son of man, take a tablet and etch Jerusalem on it. 2 Lay a siege against it with a siege wall and ramp, pitch camps, and position battering rams all around it. 3 Then set up an iron plate as a wall between you and the city. Face it and press the siege against it. It’s an omen for Israel.

item.title: Ezekiel 5
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 5

Ezek 4:9-17

1 “Son of man, take a sharp sword and shave off your hair and beard. Divide up the hair on a scales. 2 Burn a third of it in the center of the city when the siege is over. Strike a third of it with a sword all around the city. Scatter another third of it in the wind, because I’m going to chase my people with a drawn sword. 3 Attach a few to the hem of your robe. 4 Throw a few in the fire. They’ll spread from the fire to the whole House of Israel.  5 I’ve set Jerusalem in the center of the nations. 6 It rebelled against my ordinances and statutes more than those other nations did.”

item.title: Ezekiel 6
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 6

A Prophecy Against the Mountains of Israel

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, face Israel’s mountains and prophesy, 3 ‘Listen to the Lord GOD’s message to the mountains and hills, ravines and valleys: “I’m going to bring a sword against you and destroy your high places. 4 Your altars will be demolished and your incense altars smashed. You’ll fall slain in front of your idols, 5 and I’ll scatter your bones around your altars. 6 Everywhere you live, towns will be waste, high places desolate, idols broken, incense altars cut down, and the religious objects you’ve made, destroyed. 7 You’ll know I’m the LORD.

item.title: Ezekiel 7
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 7

Ezek 6:11-14

The End Has Come

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, the Lord GOD says to Israel, ‘The end is coming on the four corners of your land. 3 I’ll judge you by your ways, direct my anger at you, and bring your detestable things against you. 4 I won’t pity or spare when I bring on you the consequences of your deeds with the detestable things you’ve used.’ 5 A disaster—a unique disaster—is coming! 6 The end is here! It has awakened against you! 7 You’re doomed, resident in the land—confusion instead of happy shouting on the mountains. [ 8 ] 9 You’ll know that I the LORD am striking you.

item.title: Ezekiel 8
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 8

Idolatry in the Temple

1 The fifth day, sixth month, sixth year, I was sitting in my house with Judah’s elders sitting in front of me, and the hand of the Lord GOD came down on me. 2 I saw what looked like a man. From the waist down, he looked like fire; from the waist up, he looked like bright glowing metal. 3 He reached out with a hand and took hold of my hair. The Spirit lifted me up between earth and sky, and took me in a vision to Jerusalem, to the entrance of the north gate of the inner court, to the seat of the idol that provokes jealousy. 4 The glory of Israel’s God was there—like what I’d seen in the plain.

item.title: Ezekiel 9
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 9

Idolaters Killed

1 He shouted, “Executioners, come here with your weapons.” 2 Six men, each holding a shattering weapon, came from the direction of the upper gate, which faces north. One of them was wearing linen with a scribe’s inkhorn on his hip. They went in and stood beside the bronze altar.

3 The glory of God rose up from the cherub it rested on and went to the threshold of the temple. He called out to the man with the inkhorn,

item.title: Ezekiel 10
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 10

The LORD’s Glory Leaves the Temple

1 Then in the expanse above the cherubs, I saw something like a sapphire stone shaped like a throne. 2 He spoke to the man dressed in linen, “Go between the turning wheels under the cherubs and fill your hands with coals. Scatter them over the city.” I watched him go in.

3 The cherubs were standing on the right side of the Temple when he went in, and a cloud filled the inner court. 4 The glory of the LORD rose up from the cherubs to the threshold of the Temple. The cloud filled the Temple, and the bright glory of the LORD filled the court. 5 The cherubs’ wings could be heard all the way to the outer court—like God Almighty when he speaks.

item.title: Ezekiel 11
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 11

Judgment on Israel’s Leaders

1 The Spirit lifted me up and took me to the east gate of the Temple. There were 25 men at the entrance of the gate, including Jaazaniah son of Azzur and Pelatiah son of Benaiah, leaders of the people. 2 He told me,

“Those men devise sin and give bad advice in this city. 3 They say, ‘Isn’t it time to build houses? This city is the pot and we’re the meat.’ 4 So prophesy against them, son of man, prophesy!”

item.title: Ezekiel 12
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 12

Judah’s Exile Symbolized

12:1A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, you’re living with a rebellious people. They have eyes but don’t see, and ears but don’t hear. 3 So get together baggage for exile and go into exile in the daytime so they can watch; go from one place to another. Maybe they’ll understand, though they’re a rebellious people. 4 Bring your baggage out in the daytime. Then go out in the evening as if you’re going into exile. 5 Make a hole in the wall so they can watch you go out through it.

item.title: Ezekiel 13
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 13

False Prophets Condemned

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, prophesy against Israel’s prophets who prophesy their own ideas, 3 ‘The Lord GOD says, “Misfortune awaits foolish prophets that follow their own imagination and haven’t seen anything. 4 Israel, your prophets have been like foxes among ruins. 5 You haven’t gone up into the breaches or built the wall around the House of Israel to stand in the battle in the day of the LORD. 6 They see falsehood and lying fortunetelling who say, ‘The LORD says,’ when he hasn’t sent them. They’re just hoping their message will be what happens. 7 Didn’t you see a false vision and say a lying fortunetelling when you said, ‘the LORD says’? I’m not the one that spoke.”’”

item.title: Ezekiel 14
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 14

Israel’s Idolatrous Leaders Condemned

1 Then some elders of Israel came and sat down in front of me. 2 A message from the LORD came to me:

3 “Son of man, these people have set up idols in their hearts, and put their sins in front of them to trip over. Should they consult me? 4 So tell them, ‘The Lord GOD says, “If any Israelites or immigrants in Israel set up idols in their heart, put their sin in front of them to trip over, and then come to a prophet to inquire from me, I’ll answer them myself because of their many idols. 5 I’ll do it to lay hold of the hearts in Israel that are alienated from me by their idols.”’ Ezek 14:1-5

item.title: Ezekiel 15
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 15

Jerusalem, a Useless Vine

         15:1A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, how does the wood in a grapevine compare to the wood in a tree limb? 3 Can you make anything out of grapevine wood, say, a peg to hang a bucket on? 4 If it’s been put in the fire for fuel, is burned on both ends and charred in the middle, can you make anything out of it? 5 If it’s intact and you can’t make it into anything, how much less when fire has burned and charred it.” 6 So the Lord GOD says, “Like grapevine wood that I’ve put in the fire for fuel, I’ve given up the people in Jerusalem. 7 Though they’ve come out of the fire, it’s charred them. You’ll know I’m the LORD when I set myself against them. 8 I’ll make the country desolate because they’ve been unfaithful.”

item.title: Ezekiel 16
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 16

Jerusalem, an Unfaithful Wife

         16:1A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, confront Jerusalem about her detestable things. 3 Say, ‘Your birth was from the Canaanites—an Amorite father and a Hittite mother. 4 When you were born, your umbilical cord wasn’t cut off, you weren’t washed, or rubbed with salt, or even wrapped in cloth. 5 Nobody took pity on you to do any of those things for you. You were tossed out in an open field because you were repulsive.

item.title: Ezekiel 17
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 17

Two Eagles and a Vine

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, tell Israel this parable. 3 ‘A large eagle with big wings, long pinions, and full plumage of many colors flew to Lebanon and plucked off 4 the top of a cedar tree. He took it to a land filled with merchants and planted it in a city full of tradesmen. 5 He also took a seedling from the land and planted it in fertile soil, like a willow beside a stream. 6 It took root and became a low, spreading vine with branches turned upward toward the eagle and roots growing down into the soil. It put out shoots and grew branches.

item.title: Ezekiel 18
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 18

The Soul That Sins Will Die

          18:1A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “What do you mean by the proverb about Israel,

         ‘The parents ate sour grapes,

      and the children’s mouths puckered’?

3 As I live, you won’t be using that proverb in Israel anymore. 4 Every soul belongs to me, the parent’s as well as the children’s. The soul that sins is the one that dies.

item.title: Ezekiel 19
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 19

A Funeral Song for Israel’s Princes

1 “Sing this funeral song for Israel’s officials:

            2 ‘What was your mother?

                  A lioness among lions!

                  She lay down among young lions;

                  she raised her cubs.

            3 When she brought up one of her cubs,

item.title: Ezekiel 20
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 20

A Dirge                                                                          Ezek 19:13-14

Israel’s Rebellion

1 The tenth day, fifth month, seventh year, some of Israel’s elders came to inquire from the LORD and sat in front of me. 2 A message from him came to me:

3 “Son of man, tell Israel’s elders the Lord GOD says, ‘Are you coming to inquire from me? As I live, I won’t let you.’

item.title: Ezekiel 21
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 21

Ezek 20:45-49

Babylon, God’s Sword of Judgment

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, face Jerusalem. Speak against its sanctuaries and prophesy against Israel, 3 ‘The LORD says, “I’ll draw my sword and slay the good and the bad in you, 4 everybody from south to north. 5 Everyone will know I’ve drawn it, and it won’t go back into its sheath.”’

item.title: Ezekiel 22
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 22

Israel’s Sins

     22:1A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, are you willing to judge the bloody city? Then tell it about its abominations. 3 Say, ‘The Lord GOD says,

                  “A city that sheds blood in it,

item.title: Ezekiel 23
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 23

The Adultery of Oholah and Oholibah

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, there were two women, daughters of the same mother. 3 They carried on prostitution in Egypt when they were young. Their breasts were pressed there and their virgin bosom was handled. 4 Their names were Oholah the older and Oholibah her sister. They became mine and had children. Samaria is Oholah; Jerusalem is Oholibah.

item.title: Ezekiel 24
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 24

That way you’ll know I’m the Lord GOD.’”                     Ezek 23:43-49

The Cooking Pot

         24:1The tenth day, tenth month, ninth year, a message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, write down the date. Today the king of Babylon laid siege to Jerusalem. 3 Tell the rebellious people a parable, ‘The Lord GOD says,

item.title: Ezekiel 25
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 25

Ezek 24:25-27

A Message for Ammon

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Face the Ammonites and prophesy against them, 3 ‘Because you said, “Ha!” at my sanctuary when it was profaned, and at Israel when it became a wasteland, and at Judah when they went into exile, 4 I’m going to turn you over to the people from the east. They’ll set up their camps among you and live there, eat your fruit and drink your milk. 5 I’ll turn Rabbah into a pasture for camels and the Ammonites a resting place for flocks. 6 Because you clapped your hands and stamped your feet and celebrated with scorn at Israel, 7 I’ll give you to the nations for spoil, cut you off from the peoples, and make you perish from the land so you’ll know I’m the LORD.’

item.title: Ezekiel 26
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 26

A Message for Tyre

1 The first day of the month, eleventh year, a message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, Tyre has said about Jerusalem, ‘Ha! its gate’s broken; it has swung open to me. I’ll be filled now that it’s laid waste.’ 3 But the Lord GOD says, ‘I’ll bring nations against you, Tyre, like waves in the sea. 4 They’ll destroy your walls and tear down your towers. I’ll scrape away the rubble and make you a bare rock, 5 a place for spreading nets by the sea. I’ve spoken: you’ll become spoil for the nations, 6 and your inhabitants on the mainland will die by the sword. You’ll know I’m the LORD.’”

item.title: Ezekiel 27
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 27

A Lament for Tyre

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, sing a funeral song for Tyre 3 that sits at the port to the sea, the nations’ merchant to many ports, The Lord GOD says,  

‘Tyre, you’ve said “I’m perfectly beautiful.”

item.title: Ezekiel 28
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 28

A Message for Tyre’s King

1 A message from the LORD came to me again:

         2“Son of man, tell the leader of Tyre, ‘The Lord GOD says,

            “You’re arrogant; you’ve said,

                   ‘I’m a god; I sit in God’s seat

                        in the heart of the sea.’

item.title: Ezekiel 29
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 29

A Message for Egypt

1 The twelfth day, tenth month, tenth year, a message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, turn toward Pharaoh, Egypt’s king, and prophesy against him and Egypt. 3 Say,’The Lord GOD says,

 “I’m against you, Pharaoh, Egypt’s king,

item.title: Ezekiel 30
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 30

A Lament for Egypt

1 A message from the LORD came to me again:

         2 “Son of man, prophesy, ‘The Lord GOD says,

            “Wail, ‘Oh for the day!’

            3 The day of the LORD is near,

item.title: Ezekiel 31
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 31

A Cedar in Lebanon

1 The first day, third month, eleventh year, a message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, tell Pharaoh and his large host,

                  ‘Who is as great as you?

      3 Assyria was a cedar in Lebanon

item.title: Ezekiel 32
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 32

A Lament for Pharaoh

1 The first day, twelfth month, twelfth year, a message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, chant a dirge for Pharaoh, Egypt’s king. Tell him,

‘You consider yourself a young lion among the nations,

      but you’re like a sea monster in the ocean.

item.title: Ezekiel 33
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 33

Ezekiel Is Israel’s Watchman

         33:1A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Tell your people, ‘If I bring the sword on a country, and the people’s watchman 3 blows the ram’s horn to warn them— 4 then those who don’t pay attention are responsible for their own death. 5 If they’d taken warning, they would’ve saved their life. 6 If the watchman sees the sword coming and doesn’t warn the people, if the sword takes some people, they’ll be taken away in their sin, but I’ll hold the watchman responsible for it.’

item.title: Ezekiel 34
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 34

Prophecy Against Israel’s Shepherds

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, prophesy against Israel’s shepherds. ‘The Lord GOD says, “Misfortune awaits you, shepherds that feed yourselves! Shouldn’t shepherds feed the flock? 3 You eat the healthy and dress with their wool. You slaughter the healthy sheep without feeding the flock. 4 You don’t strengthen the sickly, heal the diseased, bandage up the crippled, bring back the scattered, or look for the lost. With force and severity, you bully them. 5 They’re scattered all over the world because they didn’t have a shepherd; they became prey for wild animals. 6 My flock wandered around in the mountains and tall hills; nobody looked for them.

item.title: Ezekiel 35
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 35

Prophecy Against Mount Seir

1 A message from the Lord came to me:

2 “Son of man, face Mount Seir and prophesy against it, 3 ‘The Lord GOD says,

“I’m against you, Mount Seir;

      I’ll make you a wasteland.

4 I’ll lay your towns waste,

item.title: Ezekiel 36
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 36

Prophecy to the Mountains of Israel

1 “Son of man, prophesy to the mountains of Israel, ‘Listen to 2 what the Lord GOD says, “Your enemy has ridiculed you, ‘Ha!’ and ‘The everlasting mountains are ours now!’” 3 So prophesy, ‘For good reason other nations made you desolate and crushed you from every direction, took possession of you, and mocked and whispered.’” 4 So, mountains of Israel, listen to the message from the Lord GOD to the mountains and hills, ravines and valleys, desolate wastes and abandoned towns that have become prey and objects of derision. 5 In my burning jealousy, I’ve spoken against those nations, particularly Edom that took over my land with wholehearted joy and scornful attitude. I’ve sworn to drive it out to become prey. 6 So prophesy to Israel’s mountains and hills, ravines and valleys, ‘I’ve spoken in my anger because you’ve had to endure the insults from other nations. 7 I swear they will endure insults.’”

item.title: Ezekiel 37
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 37

A Valley of Dry Bones

1-2 The LORD’s hand was on me. He took me out in spirit and put me down in a valley full of dry bones, 2 and had me walk around among them.

3 “Son of man, can these bones come alive?”

“Lord GOD, you know.”

item.title: Ezekiel 38
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 38

A Message for Gog

1 A message from the LORD came to me:

2 “Son of man, face Gog of the land of Magog, the prince that rules Meshech, and Tubal, and prophesy against him, 3 ‘The Lord GOD says, “I’m against you, Gog. 4 I’ll turn you around, put hooks in your jaws, and take you out—you, your army, horses and horsemen, all splendidly attired, a large army with buckler and shield, wielding swords; 5 Persia, Nubia, and Put with them, all with shield and helmet; 6 Gomer with all its troops; Beth-togarmah from the remote parts of the north with all its troops—many nations with you.

item.title: Ezekiel 39
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 39

1 “Son of man, prophesy against Gog, ‘The Lord GOD says, “I’m against you, ruler of Meshech, and Tubal. 2 I’ll turn you around, drive you on, take you up from the most remote parts in the north, and bring you against Israel. 3 I’ll knock the bow out of your left hand and the arrows out of your right hand. 4-5 You and your troops will fall on the mountains in Israel. I’ll feed you to every vulture and wild animal in the open country. I’ve said so. 6 I’ll rain down fire on Magog and those who live securely in distant lands. They’ll know I’m the LORD.’  Ezek 39:1-6

item.title: Ezekiel 40
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 40

VISION OF THE NEW TEMPLE AREA

1 At the beginning of the twenty-fifth year of our exile, tenth month, fourteenth year after the city fell, the LORD’s hand weighed heavy on me. 2 In visions he took me to Israel and set me on a tall mountain. On it to the south was a structure like a city. 3 He took me over there. Standing in the gate was a bronze-looking man with a flax line and a measuring rod in his hand. 4 He told me,

item.title: Ezekiel 41
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 41

1 Then he took me to the nave and measured the side pillars. Each pillar was 10' 4" 6 cubits square. 2 The entrance was 17' 3" 10 cubits wide; the sides of the entrance were 8' 7" 3 cubits on each side. He measured the length and width of the nave at 69' x 34' 6" 40 x 20 cubits. 3 He went inside and measured each side pillar of the doorway at 3' 5" 2 cubits, and the doorway at 6' 10" high and 12' 1" wide 6h x 7w cubits. 4 He measured the front of the nave at 34' 6" 20 cubits square He told me, “This is the Holy Place.”

item.title: Ezekiel 42
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 42

Rooms for the Priests

1 Then he took me north to the outer court, to the rooms across from the Temple yard and the north building. 2 The length of the building on the north side was 172' 5" x 86' 3" 50 x 100 cubits. 3 Beside the 34' 6" 20 cubits that belonged to the inner court and facing the pavement of the outer court, there was a three-story gallery. 4 In front of its rooms was an inner walkway 17' 3" wide x 172' 5" long 10 x 100lcubits with doorways on the north. 5 The upper rooms were narrower, because the galleries took more space away from them than from the lower and middle floors of the building. 6 The rooms had three stories and didn’t have any pillars like the pillars in the courts. So the upper rooms were set back more than the lower two levels. 7 The outer wall beside the rooms toward the court was 86' 3" 50 cubits long. 8 The rooms in the outer court were 86' 3" 50 cubits long. The rooms facing the Temple were 172' 5" 100 cubits long. 9 Below those rooms, was the east entrance as you enter them from the outer court, where the outside wall begins.

item.title: Ezekiel 43
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 43

The LORD’s Glory Returns to the Temple

1 Then he led me to the east gate, 2 and the glory of Israel’s God was shining through it. His voice sounded like crashing water, and the earth radiated with his glory. 3 The vision I saw was like the one when he came to destroy the city, like the one by the Chebar River. I fell on my face. 4 The glory of the LORD came into the Temple through the east gate. 5 The Spirit lifted me up and took me to the inner court, and the glory of the LORD filled the Temple. Ezek 43:1-5

item.title: Ezekiel 44
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 44

The Prince, Levites, and Priests

1 Then he brought me back through the outer gate of the sanctuary, which faces east; it was shut. 2 The LORD told me,

“Keep this gate shut. Nobody is to go in through it because I’ve gone in through it. 3 The prince will sit in it to eat bread in my presence. He’ll go in by the vestibule of the gate and go out the same way.

item.title: Ezekiel 45
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 45

Division of the Land

1 “When you divide the land by lot for inheritance, allot to the LORD a holy district 8.2 x 3¼ miles 25,000 x 10,000 cubits; it will be holy throughout. 2 Inside it will be a holy place 861' 500 cubits square with 86' 3" 50 cubits open all the way around. 3 Inside the district, set aside a section 8.2 x 3¼ miles 25,000 x 10,000 cubits for the sanctuary, the most holy place, 4 the holy portion of the land. It will be for the priests, the ministers of the sanctuary who come near to minister to the LORD, a place for their houses and a holy place for the sanctuary. 5 Another section 8.2 x 3¼ miles 25,000 x 10,000 cubits will be for the Levites, the ministers of the Temple, and as their possession for towns to live in. 6 Alongside the section designated as the holy district, assign for the possession of the city an area 1.63 x 8.2 miles 5,000 x 25,000 cubits. It will be for the whole House of Israel.                           Ezek 45:1-6

item.title: Ezekiel 46
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 46

1 ‘The Lord GOD says, “The gate in the inner court that faces east will be shut the six working days, but open on Sabbaths and new moons. 2 The prince will go in through the porch of the gate from outside and stand by the gate post. The priests will provide his burnt offering and peace offerings. He’ll worship at the threshold of the gate and then leave, but the gate won’t be shut till that evening. 3 The people will also worship at the doorway of that gate before the LORD on Sabbaths and new moons. 4 The prince’s burnt offering on the Sabbath will be 6 lambs and 1 ram without blemish. 5 The grain offering will be a 4½ pecks 1 ephah with the ram, and a grain offering with the lambs as much as he can give, and 1½ gallons 1 hin of olive oil with each 4½ pecks 1 ephah.  6 On the new moon, he’ll offer 1 young bull, 6 lambs, and 1 ram, all without blemish. 7 He’ll provide a grain offering, 4½ pecks 1 ephah with the bull and the ram, and as much as he can give with the lambs, and 1½ gallons 1 hin of olive oil for each 4½ pecks 1 ephah. 8 The prince will enter through the porch of the gate and leave the same way.                            Ezek 46:1-8

item.title: Ezekiel 47
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 47

A River of Healing from the Temple

1 Then he took me back to the Temple door. Water was running east from under the threshold of the Temple, which faced east. It was running down from under the right side of the Temple from south of the altar. 2 He brought me out through the north gate and led me around the outside of the outer gate by way of the gate that faces east. Water was trickling from the south side.

item.title: Ezekiel 48
item.description:
Ezekiel Chapter 48

1 “These are the names of the tribes. Beginning on the northern boundary, from the sea by way of Hethlon to Lebo-hamath, as far as Hazar-enon (on the northern boundary of Damascus beside Hamath) running from the east side to the west: one portion each for Dan, 2 Asher, 3 Naphtali, 4 Manasseh, 5 Ephraim, 6 Reuben, 7 and Judah.
